pattern: Because prefix XOR is its own inverse, each original element is recovered by XORing consecutive prefix values, undoing the running accumulation.
hints: The first original value equals the first prefix value, since nothing precedes it.
XOR has the cancellation property a ^ a = 0, so applying it to two adjacent prefixes strips away everything they share.
For i > 0, arr[i] = pref[i] ^ pref[i-1]; the shared earlier terms cancel, leaving exactly the i-th element.

edge_inputs.description: prefix repeats a value, original element is zero there
increasing prefixes do not mean increasing originals

stress.description: long random prefix-xor array across the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    pref = [rng.randint(0, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [pref]
stress.note: any non-negative bounded array is a valid prefix-xor input

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def findArray(self, pref):
        # Independent method: maintain the running xor of the answer so far and pick
        # each element as the value that makes the running xor equal the target prefix.
        arr = []
        running = 0
        for target in pref:
            elem = running ^ target  # value needed so running ^ elem == target
            arr.append(elem)
            running = running ^ elem
        return arr
